assumptions: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable/payable is treated as an intragroup monetary item for purposes of considering the IFRS 9 monetary-item exception.
The question is assessed in ordinary consolidated financial statements, not in the special investment-entity scenario referenced in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5.

approaches.label: General intragroup prohibition
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 sets the default rule that, in consolidated financial statements, hedged items must generally be with parties external to the reporting entity.

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 provides a specific exception allowing foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item to be designated if the exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Separate financial statements hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 distinguishes consolidated accounting from individual or separate financial statements, where intragroup transactions may still be designated.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 describe a separate foreign currency hedge model for net investments in foreign operations, which defines an important boundary distinct from hedging an intragroup receivable.
